title: Presepsin level in predicting patients' in-hospital mortality from sepsis under sepsis-3 criteria
study_design: Prospective observational study with retrospective analysis

sample_size.source_quote: Overall, 138 patients were included in this study.

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Overall 138 patients met the inclusion criteria, including 74 survivors and 64 non-survivors.

mortality_predictors.source_quote: Binary logistic regression showed that the presepsin level (Odds ratio (OR) =1.221, 95% conﬁdence interval (CI): 1.024–1.455, P=0.026)... The best prognostic cutoff point for presepsin was 2,623 pg/mL, at which level the sensitivity and speciﬁcity were 62.71% (95% CI: 49.15% to 74.96%) and 72.6% (95% CI: 60.91% to 82.39%), respectively.

Binary logistic regression showed that the presepsin level (Odds ratio (OR) =1.221, 95% conﬁdence interval (CI): 1.024–1.455, P=0.026), lactate level (OR =1.296, 95% CI: 1.071–1.569, P=0.008) and SOFA score (OR =1.404, 95% CI: 1.209–1.632, P=0.000) were risk factors for patients' in-hospital mortality from sepsis.

SOFA score (OR =1.404, 95% CI: 1.209–1.632, P=0.000) were risk factors for patients' in-hospital mortality from sepsis... The SOFA score had the best performance with the highest AUC value (AUC =0.793, P=0.000).

the AUC value of a combination of the SOFA score and the presepsin level was signiﬁcantly higher than that of the SOFA score alone in predicting patients' in-hospital mortality (AUC: 0.817 vs 0.793, P=0.041).
there was no association between patients' in-hospital mortality and their PCT levels (OR =0.995, 95% CI: 0.957–1.089, P=0.527)
